key_insight: The paper derives exact worst- and best-case distorted utility values under moment ambiguity by solving the inner distributional-robust problem in quantile space with nonsmooth optimality conditions and isotonic projection.
engineering_relevance: This gives a scalable oracle for robust decisions under uncertainty, enabling capacity, pricing, inventory, and contract design that remain feasible even when only a few moments of the payoff distribution are known.
method: Reformulate the extreme-case distorted utility problem in the quantile domain.
Use nonsmooth variational analysis to obtain exact first-order optimality conditions and closed-form extremal values/distributions.
Handle the monotonicity constraint via isotonic projection onto the monotone cone, yielding a linear-time discretized inner solve.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports recovery and extension of classical moment bounds in three worked examples and a real capacity-provisioning case for generative AI inference that shows reduced required capacity while preserving service compliance.
watch_item: The approach assumes moment information and a specified distortion/utility form; discretization and isotonic projection must be implemented carefully for nonsmooth or atomic cases.
paper_title: Extreme-Case Distorted Utility under Moment Ambiguity
